tasks.phaseName: Phase A — AI consumption (current fumadocs, PR 1)
tasks.subject: [agent-consumable-docs] [PA] A.5 — Scaffold components/ai/page-actions.tsx
tasks.description:
Scaffold the local, customizable page-action client components. Run: npx @fumadocs/cli add ai/page-actions — this writes local client components into the project (the point is LOCAL OWNERSHIP; keep the scaffold output as-authored). Target path apps/site/src/components/ai/page-actions.tsx.

The scaffold exports LLMCopyButton and ViewOptions, which wrap the ui primitives MarkdownCopyButton({ markdownUrl }) and ViewOptionsPopover({ markdownUrl, githubUrl }) from fumadocs-ui/layouts/shared/page-actions (present at the installed ui 16.7.16 — node_modules/fumadocs-ui/dist/layouts/shared/page-actions.d.ts). It is a client module ('use client') that the server docs page renders with string props (task 1.6). The ViewOptions menu ships (from the fumadocs source): Copy Markdown (client fetch(markdownUrl) → clipboard), View as Markdown (link to markdownUrl), Open in GitHub (githubUrl), Open in Claude (claude.ai/new?q=...), and additional deep-links (Open in ChatGPT, Scira, Cursor).

HONESTY (Decision D5): keep the scaffold's plain menu labels ("Open in Claude", "View as Markdown", "Open in GitHub"). "Open in Claude" is a claude.ai WEB deep-link (opens claude.ai in a new tab with a prompt pre-filled to read the page's URL) — it is NOT a Claude Code or desktop link. Do not add hype or imply an integration that isn't there. Add TSDoc to the exports (Hard Rule 4) if the scaffold omits it.

FILES: apps/site/src/components/ai/page-actions.tsx (new, scaffolded — keep as-authored; add TSDoc only if missing).

ACCEPTANCE: components/ai/page-actions.tsx exists exporting LLMCopyButton and ViewOptions wired to the fumadocs-ui page-actions primitives; TSDoc present on the exports; typecheck + lint pass; menu labels are the plain honest labels (no hype).

VERIFY: pnpm --filter @dorkos/site typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/site lint.

DEPENDENCIES: none. PARALLELIZABLE with 1.1 (config) and 1.2 (helper) — disjoint files. Consumed by 1.6 (page.tsx imports LLMCopyButton + ViewOptions). Spec cite: Detailed Design A.5 (scaffold + kept as-authored), User Experience (honest "Open in Claude" framing, D5), Non-Goals (no Claude Code / desktop deep link).

tasks.phaseName: Phase A — AI consumption (current fumadocs, PR 1)
tasks.subject: [agent-consumable-docs] [PA] A.6 — Insert page-action row in page.tsx (D-A1 gate) + Phase A build gate + changelog
tasks.description:
The Phase A confluence: insert the action row into the catch-all docs page, then run the real Phase A acceptance (the BUILD gate) across all pieces, and write the user-facing changelog fragment. This is the serial tail of Phase A — the helper (1.2), both routes (1.3/1.4), the config flag (1.1), and the scaffold (1.5) must all be in place first. (1) INSERT THE ACTION ROW. Edit apps/site/src/app/(docs)/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx (an async Server Component). It currently imports source, DocsPage/DocsBody/DocsTitle/DocsDescription, getMDXComponents, APIPage — and does NOT yet import siteConfig or the scaffolded components. Add imports: import { siteConfig } from '@/config/site'; and import { LLMCopyButton, ViewOptions } from '@/components/ai/page-actions'; . Compute inside the Page function: const markdownUrl = `${page.url}.mdx`; const githubUrl = `${siteConfig.github}/blob/main/docs/${page.path}`; Render the client row AFTER and BEFORE , gated on prose: {!isGeneratedApiPage(page) && (
)} markdownUrl = page.url + ".mdx" → the 1.3 route (keep in agreement with whatever segment 1.3 shipped). githubUrl (O2, wire it) = siteConfig.github + "/blob/main/docs/" + page.path; page.path is the file path relative to the docs/ content dir (Page.path — fumadocs-core/dist/index-gTnj-v3t.d.ts:280,286); siteConfig.github is https://github.com/dork-labs/dorkos (config/site.ts:13). Use the plain canonical github URL (no UTM), matching llms.txt. D-A1 GATE (Sub-decision): render the row ONLY when the page is NOT a generated API page — reuse the SAME predicate extracted in 1.4 (isGeneratedApiPage from '@/lib/is-generated-api-page'), so A.4 and A.6 share one signal. Generated API pages have no prose to copy, so a Copy-Markdown there would be a dead affordance; the raw .mdx route still serves those pages (harmless), only the buttons are hidden. Keep the server→client boundary clean: only strings (markdownUrl, githubUrl) cross into the client components. (2) PHASE A BUILD GATE (the real acceptance). Run pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build, then assert: a /docs/.mdx exists and is text/markdown (curl a built page under next start, or inspect the static output); /llms-full.txt exists, is text/plain, contains a hand-authored page's title and does NOT contain a generated API operation's line; a rendered docs page shows the Copy-Markdown + View-Options row above the body, and an API operation page does NOT. Plus typecheck + lint green. NO dependency bump in this phase (confirm apps/site/package.json fumadocs versions are unchanged: core/ui ^16.7.16, openapi ~10.6.8, mdx ^14.3.0). If the build rejects the [[...slug]].mdx segment, apply the 1.3 fallback placement and update markdownUrl here to match. (3) CHANGELOG FRAGMENT (### Added — the on-brand headline). Mint a timestamp id with node --experimental-strip-types .claude/scripts/id.ts (YYMMDD-HHMMSS) and create changelog/unreleased/-agent-consumable-docs.md (NO frontmatter; Keep-a-Changelog headings; writing-for-humans voice — plain enough for a smart 9th grader who doesn't code; honest, no hype). Convey the user benefit plainly, e.g. under "### Added": "Read any doc as clean markdown, or pull the whole docs set into your agent in one fetch. Every docs page is now fetchable as markdown (add .mdx to any docs URL), a new /llms-full.txt gives your agent the whole hand-written corpus in one request, and a quiet action row above each page lets you copy the markdown or open the page in Claude. (DOR-165)". Note the honest "Open in Claude" framing (opens claude.ai web, not Claude Code). This single fragment carries Phase A's user-facing value; Phase B carries its own only if a user-visible change results (see task 2.6). FILES: apps/site/src/app/(docs)/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx (edit), changelog/unreleased/-agent-consumable-docs.md (new). ACCEPTANCE: the action row renders above the body on hand-authored pages and is hidden on generated API pages (D-A1); markdownUrl = page.url + ".mdx" and githubUrl = siteConfig.github + "/blob/main/docs/" + page.path; the Phase A build gate passes (both routes emit correct static bytes, the row shows/ hides correctly); typecheck + lint green; NO dependency bump; a valid ### Added changelog fragment exists with a timestamp-id filename and passes the writing-for-humans checks. VERIFY: pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build && pnpm --filter @dorkos/site typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/site lint (then the manual static-bytes / next start checks above for the two routes and the action row). DEPENDENCIES: 1.1 (flag needed for the build to emit processed markdown), 1.3 (the .mdx route markdownUrl targets), 1.4 (the extracted predicate reused for D-A1, and the llms-full.txt route the build gate asserts), 1.5 (the scaffolded components imported here). Transitively 1.2. NOT parallelizable — it is the Phase A integration + build-gate + changelog tail. Spec cite: Detailed Design A.6 (verbatim insertion + D-A1), Testing Strategy (Phase A build gate), Documentation (changelog fragment), Acceptance Criteria (Phase A, action-row + build + no-bump + "Open in Claude" honesty checkboxes).
tasks.activeForm: Inserting the action row, running the Phase A build gate, and writing the changelog

tasks.phaseName: Phase B — fumadocs 16.10 / openapi 11 upgrade (gated, PR 2)
tasks.subject: [agent-consumable-docs] [PB] B.0 — Reproduce the /docs/api/* prerender failure on a throwaway install (the gate, FIRST)
tasks.description:
Phase B's FIRST execute step is reproduction, not a code edit. Done-ness here is the CAPTURED ERROR + a written O1 resolution — the gate, not the D6 hypothesis. Everything else in Phase B (2.1–2.6) is downstream of this.

On a THROWAWAY install (a scratch worktree or scratch branch — do NOT commit), bump the four welded deps in apps/site/package.json to their Phase B targets (fumadocs-core ^16.10, fumadocs-ui ^16.10, fumadocs-openapi ^11, and re-check fumadocs-mdx against core 16.10), run pnpm install, then run pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build and CAPTURE the actual /docs/api/* prerender error verbatim. Confirm (or correct) the D6 leading hypothesis: openapi 11 makes APIPage a client component ("requires api-page.tsx to be a client component"; "server should pass page props using page.data.getOpenAPIPageProps()"), while today apps/site/src/components/api-page.tsx carries the opposite invariant (a load-bearing comment: "This file must NOT have 'use client' — APIPage is an async Server Component ... that performs file I/O to load the spec"), and the 65 generated MDX pages pass the spec as a file-path string (<APIPage document={"../../docs/api/openapi.json"} operations={[...]} /> — docs/api/api/tasks/post.mdx:14). The hypothesis: a v11 client APIPage cannot read that relative filesystem path at prerender/hydration, so /docs/api/* fails to prerender. Also confirm next 16.2.9 / react 19.2.5 peers are satisfied by openapi 11 and note whether fumadocs-mdx needs a peer bump (resolve from the actual lockfile, not assumed).

ADAPTATION BOUNDARY (encode this): the migration's target design in 2.2–2.4 (client APIPage + getOpenAPIPageProps bridge) is the LEADING hypothesis. Phase B is DONE when its GATE is green — pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build succeeds including /docs/api/* prerender, /docs/api renders interactively, and openapi-fresh is green — REGARDLESS of whether the exact fix matches D6. If the reproduced root cause differs, adapt the fix within that gate and RECORD the correction: update Open Question O1 in specs/agent-consumable-docs/02-specification.md (it is already marked RESOLVED with the reproduce-first answer; append the OBSERVED root cause) and drop an agent/assumption breadcrumb via the tracker adapter. Discard the throwaway install once the error is captured; the real bump is committed in 2.1.

FILES: none committed (throwaway install; the deliverable is the captured error + the written O1 resolution note appended to the spec's Open Questions).

ACCEPTANCE: the real /docs/api/* prerender failure is reproduced on a throwaway install and its root cause is confirmed in writing (or the D6 hypothesis corrected in writing) BEFORE any Phase B fix is committed; the four-dep peer picture (mdx/next/react) is recorded. This satisfies the Phase B Acceptance Criteria first checkbox.

VERIFY: pnpm install then pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build in the throwaway install; capture the prerender error text; confirm/correct D6 and write the O1 note.

DEPENDENCIES: none. GATES all of Phase B (2.1–2.6 are downstream). This task is the second PR's opening move — see TWO-PR PLAN in 03-tasks.md. Spec cite: Detailed Design B.0 (reproduce-first + adaptation boundary), Open Questions O1 (RESOLVED — reproduce-first), Acceptance Criteria (Phase B, first checkbox).
tasks.activeForm: Reproducing the prerender failure on a throwaway install

tasks.phaseName: Phase B — fumadocs 16.10 / openapi 11 upgrade (gated, PR 2)
tasks.subject: [agent-consumable-docs] [PB] B.1 — Welded dependency bump (core/ui ^16.10, openapi ^11) + lockfile
tasks.description:
Commit the welded version bump — the two bumps are inseparable: fumadocs-openapi@11 peer-requires fumadocs-core@^16.10 and fumadocs-ui@^16.10, and openapi 10 peer-caps core below 16.10, so you cannot move one without the other (this is exactly why the earlier deps PR reverted both).

Edit apps/site/package.json: fumadocs-core ^16.7.16 → ^16.10, fumadocs-ui ^16.7.16 → ^16.10, fumadocs-openapi ~10.6.8 → ^11, in ONE change. Re-check fumadocs-mdx (currently ^14.3.0) against core 16.10 and bump ONLY if the resolved peer range demands it (decide from the resolved lockfile in 2.0, not assumed). Confirm next 16.2.9 / react 19.2.5 peers stay satisfied (already checked in 2.0). Run pnpm install and update the pnpm lockfile (pnpm-lock.yaml).

FILES: apps/site/package.json (edit), pnpm-lock.yaml (updated by pnpm install).

ACCEPTANCE: apps/site/package.json pins fumadocs-core/fumadocs-ui at ^16.10 and fumadocs-openapi at ^11 (and fumadocs-mdx bumped only if required); the lockfile is updated; next/react peers satisfied; pnpm install completes without peer errors. (The site will NOT fully build until 2.2–2.5 land — that is expected mid-migration; do not treat the intermediate red build as a failure of this task.)

VERIFY: pnpm install (clean peer resolution) && node -e "const p=require('./apps/site/package.json');console.log(p.dependencies['fumadocs-core'],p.dependencies['fumadocs-ui'],p.dependencies['fumadocs-openapi'])".

DEPENDENCIES: 2.0 (the reproduction confirms the exact peer picture before the bump is committed). Opens the two independent Phase B branches: branch 1 runtime wiring (2.2 → 2.3) and branch 2 generator/artifacts (2.4 → 2.5), which run in parallel. Spec cite: Detailed Design B.1, Technical Dependencies (Phase B welded bump), Acceptance Criteria (Phase B, versions + lockfile checkbox).
tasks.activeForm: Bumping the welded fumadocs dependencies and lockfile

tasks.phaseName: Phase B — fumadocs 16.10 / openapi 11 upgrade (gated, PR 2)
tasks.subject: [agent-consumable-docs] [PB] B.2+B.3 — Move createAPIPage to a client APIPage module (openapi.ts split + api-page.tsx becomes client)
tasks.description:
One atomic compile-unit spanning two files: under openapi 11, createAPIPage produces a CLIENT component and must be invoked from a client module, so the factory call moves out of the server file lib/openapi.ts into the client file components/api-page.tsx. Splitting this across two commits would break the build in between, so it is a single task.

(B.2) apps/site/src/lib/openapi.ts — createOpenAPI({ input: ['../../docs/api/openapi.json'] }) STAYS (v11 keeps the server schema loader here; input as a file-path array remains valid server input). Under v11, createAPIPage's UI options (mediaAdapters, shikiOptions, playground.enabled) move to the client and DorkOS passes NONE today, so no options object is needed — but the createAPIPage factory call must now live in a client module. REMOVE the createAPIPage(openapi) export from lib/openapi.ts (currently line 18); keep only the server openapi instance, and (if v11 requires) expose it so the server page can call getOpenAPIPageProps (task 2.3).

(B.3) apps/site/src/components/api-page.tsx — INVERT the current invariant. Today it is a plain re-export with a load-bearing comment "This file must NOT have 'use client' — APIPage is an async Server Component ... that performs file I/O to load the spec" (api-page.tsx:1-10). Make it a 'use client' module that creates and re-exports the v11 client APIPage via createAPIPage from fumadocs-openapi/ui (bound to the openapi instance imported from '@/lib/openapi'). DELETE the "must NOT have 'use client'" comment and replace it with a comment explaining the v11 client-component requirement (why it is now a client module). The client APIPage renders from SERIALIZED props, never file I/O.

Confirm against the reproduced v11 behavior (2.0) and the v11 type surfaces: fumadocs-openapi/ui createAPIPage (v11 client factory) and fumadocs-openapi/server createOpenAPI (server loader). Respect the SDK-confinement and FSD rules (no new banned imports; api-page.tsx stays the local wrapper the catch-all page imports).

FILES: apps/site/src/lib/openapi.ts (edit — drop the APIPage factory, keep the server instance), apps/site/src/components/api-page.tsx (edit — becomes the 'use client' client APIPage module).

ACCEPTANCE: lib/openapi.ts exports only the server openapi instance (no createAPIPage there); components/api-page.tsx is a 'use client' module exporting the v11 client APIPage created via createAPIPage from fumadocs-openapi/ui; the old "must NOT have 'use client'" comment is gone and replaced with the v11 rationale; typecheck passes for these two files. Full render is proven by the 2.6 gate.

VERIFY: pnpm --filter @dorkos/site typecheck (with 2.1's deps installed).

DEPENDENCIES: 2.1 (needs openapi 11 installed). PARALLELIZABLE with branch 2 (2.4, 2.5 — disjoint files: generator + generated docs). Feeds 2.3 (the props bridge renders this client APIPage). Spec cite: Detailed Design B.2 + B.3, Background (the prerender crux / the invariant to invert), Acceptance Criteria (Phase B, client APIPage checkbox).
tasks.activeForm: Moving createAPIPage into a client APIPage module

tasks.phaseName: Phase B — fumadocs 16.10 / openapi 11 upgrade (gated, PR 2)
tasks.subject: [agent-consumable-docs] [PB] B.4 — Server→client OpenAPI props bridge in page.tsx (getOpenAPIPageProps)
tasks.description:
Wire the server catch-all page to supply the OpenAPI page props to the now-client APIPage so it no longer reads a filesystem path. v11 guidance: "server should pass page props using page.data.getOpenAPIPageProps()" (provided by openapiPlugin).

Edit apps/site/src/app/(docs)/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx. Today the generated MDX embeds <APIPage document="...path..." operations={...} /> and receives APIPage through getMDXComponents({ APIPage }) (page.tsx:47). Under v11: for API pages, the server page resolves the bundled OpenAPI props (via page.data.getOpenAPIPageProps() or the EXACT v11 accessor confirmed during the 2.0 reproduction) and passes them into the client APIPage so it renders from serialized props with NO runtime relative-path file read. The generated MDX's document prop stops being a relative file path (task 2.5 regenerates it to the v11 shape).

RESOLVED IN EXECUTE FROM THE 2.0 REPRODUCTION (adaptation boundary, not a blocker): the precise wiring — whether the document is bundled at generate-time into the MDX, injected by the server page, or both — follows the observed v11 behavior. The CONTRACT is fixed and non-negotiable: /docs/api/* prerenders AND renders interactively with NO runtime relative-path filesystem read. Note this file was also edited in Phase A task 1.6 (the action row); since Phase B is the SECOND PR sequenced after Phase A merges, you are editing the post-A page.tsx — preserve the 1.6 action-row block.

FILES: apps/site/src/app/(docs)/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx (edit).

ACCEPTANCE: the catch-all server page supplies OpenAPI page props to the client APIPage (via getOpenAPIPageProps or the confirmed v11 accessor); no code path passes a relative file-path document to a client component for runtime reading; the Phase A action-row block from 1.6 is preserved; typecheck passes. Interactive render is proven by the 2.6 gate.

VERIFY: pnpm --filter @dorkos/site typecheck (full prerender/render proof is the 2.6 gate).

DEPENDENCIES: 2.2 (the client APIPage + moved createAPIPage this bridges into). PARALLELIZABLE with branch 2 (2.4, 2.5 — disjoint files). Spec cite: Detailed Design B.4 (props bridge, contract fixed / wiring resolved in EXECUTE), Background (file-path document prop), Acceptance Criteria (Phase B, /docs/api renders + no relative-path read checkbox).
tasks.activeForm: Wiring the server→client OpenAPI props bridge

tasks.phaseName: Phase B — fumadocs 16.10 / openapi 11 upgrade (gated, PR 2)
tasks.subject: [agent-consumable-docs] [PB] B.5 — Update generate-api-docs.ts to the v11 generateFiles signature
tasks.description:
Update the generator to the v11 generateFiles signature so the regenerated MDX (task 2.5) matches the v11 shape. This is branch 2 of Phase B (generator/artifacts), independent of and parallel with the runtime-wiring branch (2.2 → 2.3).

Edit apps/site/scripts/generate-api-docs.ts. Today it calls generateFiles({ input: openapi, output: outputDir, includeDescription: true }) where openapi = createOpenAPI({ input: ['../../docs/api/openapi.json'] }) (generate-api-docs.ts:39-47). v11 changelog notes: "drop the whole-map factory () => SchemaMap; use a record instead" and generateFiles "expects an OpenAPI server instance." DorkOS already passes input: openapi (the server instance) + output + includeDescription: true, which matches the "server instance" path — so RE-VERIFY the exact v11 Config signature (fumadocs-openapi/dist/generate-file.d.ts) and update the input/options fields ONLY if their shapes changed. KEEP the pre-generation prune fs.rmSync(generatedDir, { recursive: true, force: true }) (generate-api-docs.ts:37) — it guards against orphan pages breaking prerender and is orthogonal to the version. The emitted MDX shape (the <APIPage> document prop and frontmatter) WILL likely change — that regeneration is task 2.5.

FILES: apps/site/scripts/generate-api-docs.ts (edit).

ACCEPTANCE: generate-api-docs.ts calls the v11 generateFiles with the correct v11 Config fields (input/options adjusted only where the v11 signature changed); the prune step is retained; the script typechecks and runs without error against the v11 API. (The regenerated output and its freshness gate are task 2.5.)

VERIFY: pnpm --filter @dorkos/site typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/site generate:api-docs (runs the script end-to-end; the byte-for-byte freshness check is 2.5's openapi-fresh gate).

DEPENDENCIES: 2.1 (needs openapi 11 installed to know the signature; informed by the 2.0 reproduction). PARALLELIZABLE with branch 1 (2.2, 2.3 — disjoint files). Feeds 2.5 (which runs this generator and commits the output). Spec cite: Detailed Design B.5 (generateFiles v11 signature + keep the prune), Acceptance Criteria (Phase B, regenerated to the v11 generateFiles shape checkbox).
tasks.activeForm: Updating the generator to the v11 generateFiles signature

tasks.phaseName: Phase B — fumadocs 16.10 / openapi 11 upgrade (gated, PR 2)
tasks.subject: [agent-consumable-docs] [PB] B.6 — Regenerate docs/api/api/** (65 files) to the v11 shape + hold openapi-fresh green
tasks.description:
Regenerate the 65 generated API MDX pages with the v11 generator and commit them, so the openapi-fresh CI gate stays green. This is the objective "API docs still fresh" contract.

Run pnpm docs:export-api (root script → tsx scripts/export-openapi.ts; regenerates docs/api/openapi.json from the unchanged server Zod schemas — the OpenAPI CONTENT does not change, only the generated MDX SHAPE), then pnpm --filter @dorkos/site generate:api-docs (the v11 generator from task 2.4). Commit the regenerated docs/api/api/** (65 files) plus the regenerated docs/api/openapi.json if it changed. The openapi-fresh gate (.github/workflows/docs-openapi-check.yml) regenerates both artifacts and FAILS on any diff, so the committed output must match the v11 generator BYTE-FOR-BYTE — run the two commands, commit, then re-run them and confirm git shows a CLEAN tree (deterministic). Do NOT hand-edit generated files.

FILES: docs/api/api/** (65 regenerated MDX files), docs/api/openapi.json (regenerated if changed).

ACCEPTANCE: docs/api/api/** is regenerated to the v11 generateFiles shape and committed; pnpm docs:export-api && pnpm --filter @dorkos/site generate:api-docs is deterministic (a second run leaves a clean git tree); the openapi-fresh CI gate is green (no diff). A dirty diff there is a hard fail.

VERIFY: pnpm docs:export-api && pnpm --filter @dorkos/site generate:api-docs && git status --porcelain docs/api (expect empty after commit); the openapi-fresh workflow passes in CI.

DEPENDENCIES: 2.4 (the v11 generator must be in place first). PARALLELIZABLE with branch 1 (2.2, 2.3 — disjoint files). Feeds 2.6 (the final render/build gate consumes the regenerated pages). Spec cite: Detailed Design B.6 (regenerate + hold openapi-fresh), Testing Strategy (Phase B — openapi-fresh is the objective freshness contract), Acceptance Criteria (Phase B, regenerated + committed + openapi-fresh green checkbox).
tasks.activeForm: Regenerating the API docs and holding openapi-fresh green

tasks.phaseName: Phase B — fumadocs 16.10 / openapi 11 upgrade (gated, PR 2)
tasks.subject: [agent-consumable-docs] [PB] B.7 — Verify source.ts plugin + full Phase B gate (build+render+openapi-fresh) + changelog assessment + ADR
tasks.description:
Phase B close-out and the objective gate. Both Phase B branches (runtime wiring 2.2→2.3 and generator/artifacts 2.4→2.5) must be complete; this task verifies the source plugin, runs the whole Phase B gate, honestly assesses whether a Phase B changelog is warranted, and extracts the ADR.

(B.7) VERIFY THE SOURCE PLUGIN. apps/site/src/lib/source.ts already uses openapiPlugin() from fumadocs-openapi/server (the v11-blessed plugin, not the removed transformerOpenAPI). Confirm the plugin name/options are unchanged in v11 and that it supplies the page-data method the 2.3 bridge relies on (getOpenAPIPageProps). Expected no-op; verify, do not assume. Also confirm by grep that NO removed v11 surface is used anywhere in apps/site: transformerOpenAPI, createCodeSample, generateTypeScriptSchema, the ui/client subpath, and the renames allowedUrls→allowedOrigins, groupStyle→folderStyle, disablePlayground→playground.enabled.

FULL PHASE B GATE (the only meaningful proof): (1) pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build succeeds INCLUDING /docs/api/* prerender — the exact failure reproduced in 2.0 now passes. (2) /docs/api and a sample operation page render the interactive client APIPage (manual/browser check under next start; no runtime relative-path file read). (3) openapi-fresh is green (held by 2.5; re-confirm here). (4) typecheck + lint green. If the 2.0-reproduced root cause differed from D6, confirm the adapted fix still lands the gate and that the O1 correction was recorded (2.0).

CHANGELOG ASSESSMENT (honest — Changed only if user-visible). Phase B is isolated infrastructure: if the API reference renders identically to before (expected), NO changelog fragment is warranted — the docs-engine upgrade is invisible to users; do NOT fabricate a user-facing entry for an invisible upgrade. Add a "### Changed" fragment (changelog/unreleased/<id>-...md, fresh timestamp id from .claude/scripts/id.ts, writing-for-humans voice) ONLY if a genuine user-visible difference results (e.g. the interactive API reference visibly gains or loses a capability). Default: no Phase B fragment.

ADR (extract at /adr:from-spec, or write by hand via the writing-adrs skill). Extract ONE ADR (status per the /adr:from-spec significance rubric) titled roughly "Agent-consumable docs — split the AI-consumption features from the fumadocs 16.10 / openapi 11 upgrade into two sequenced phases; migrate APIPage to a client component under openapi 11." Capture: the decoupling decision (value ships without the risky bump — Phase A/B split, D1/D2); the client-component APIPage + getOpenAPIPageProps bridge (D6); and the reproduce-first / gate-defined adaptation boundary (O1). This single ADR records the whole two-phase decision; it is extracted at the END of Phase B because the client-migration consequences are only proven once the gate is green. Add the decisions/manifest.json entry with a timestamp id from .claude/scripts/id.ts. Run /adr:from-spec specs/agent-consumable-docs/02-specification.md. No existing ADR constrains this (apps/site docs plumbing has no prior decision record; openapi-fresh is a CI convention, not an ADR).

FILES: apps/site/src/lib/source.ts (verify — expected no-op), decisions/<id>-agent-consumable-docs.md (new ADR) + decisions/manifest.json (edit), and OPTIONALLY changelog/unreleased/<id>-...md (only if a user-visible Phase B change results).

ACCEPTANCE: source.ts openapiPlugin() is confirmed v11-correct and supplies getOpenAPIPageProps; no removed v11 surface is used; pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build succeeds including /docs/api/* prerender; /docs/api renders the interactive client APIPage with no relative-path read; openapi-fresh green; typecheck + lint green; the ADR exists (decisions/<id>-<slug>.md + a valid manifest entry) capturing the decoupling + client-migration + reproduce-first decisions; a Phase B changelog fragment exists IFF a user-visible change resulted (honestly, likely none).

VERIFY: pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build && pnpm --filter @dorkos/site typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/site lint && node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('decisions/manifest.json','utf8'))" (then the manual /docs/api render check under next start; the openapi-fresh workflow in CI).

DEPENDENCIES: 2.3 (runtime wiring complete) and 2.5 (artifacts regenerated). Transitively 2.2, 2.4, 2.1, 2.0. NOT parallelizable — the Phase B integration + gate + prose tail. Spec cite: Detailed Design B.7 + Removed v11 surfaces, Testing Strategy (Phase B build + CI gate), Documentation (changelog + ADR), Related ADRs (the single two-phase ADR), Acceptance Criteria (Phase B, build+render + openapi-fresh + no-removed-surfaces checkboxes).
tasks.activeForm: Verifying the source plugin, running the Phase B gate, and extracting the ADR
